channel.title: AI on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in AI on yexca'Blog

item.title: Discussion on Audio Preprocessing for AI Cover Training
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3.5-flash Completely relying on AI to extract clean vocals is still not quite realistic with current technology; in most cases, manual correction is needed. However, as long as you have enough data, you can always scrape together enough AI-extracted vocals suitable for training. This article discusses a workflow for filtering training audio using only AI, from the perspective of an outsider. Everything used here is open-source.

item.title: AI Covers with Roformer and Applio
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-3.5-flash Getting Audio For training, real lossless audio is highly recommended (specifically pcm_f32le WAV files). It’s best to rip them from physical albums. You can refer to this guide: https://www.bilibili.com/opus/925630344961458181 , but skip the FLAC conversion and export directly to WAV. This guide uses Applio (based on the RVC architecture). You’ll need about 10 to 60 minutes of clean vocals (acapella) to get good training …

item.title: NovelAI Image Generation (WebUI)
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash-lite Deploying on Windows 11. For other systems (like Linux), refer to: AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui: Stable Diffusion web UI Disk Space Usage Program: 5.3 GiB (models not included) Runtime: 5.5 GiB+ Ensure you have at least 6 GiB free on your C: drive before running. Otherwise, your computer might freeze or crash. Environment First, your network environment. Make sure you’re connected to the internet.
